title: The Stretch of a Soul
description:
The last thing we should want to do with accounts of mystical or transcendent or contemplative experiences is send them to the taxidermist and mount them for inspection.

The post The Stretch of a Soul appeared first on The Point Magazine.
hook: On treating mystical experience like a dead animal pinned to a board—why analysis murders what matters most.

title: How Mexico’s World Cup Run Brought a Small Oregon Town to Life
description: "A little less than eighty miles from the Oregon coast and a little more than a thousand miles from the U.S.-Mexico border, Jose was selling the feeling of being around something familiar."
hook: An Oregon town discovers itself through Mexico's World Cup run—belonging isn't about where you're from, it's about who's beside you.

title: Birds of War review – boundary-breaking love story
description: Spanning 13 years of the Syrian Civil War, this documentary follows a BBC reporter and a frontline activist whose relationship unfolds entirely through their phones.
hook: A love story told entirely through phone screens during 13 years of war—intimacy reduced to light and text, made unbearably real.

title: In Kennedy Ryan's romance, happily-ever-after is for everyone
description: The romance books Ryan read growing up rarely included characters who looked like her. Now she deliberately centers people the genre has left out, including Black, Indigenous and queer women.
hook: Kennedy Ryan writes romance for people the genre abandoned—Black, queer, Indigenous women finally get to want what everyone else does.

title: Waves Eaten by Flames: Endless Summer by Fennesz at 25
description:
A quarter of a century on Jon Buckland considers the complex emotional affects triggered by Christian Fennesz' third album, Endless Summer. Contains discussion of suicide

Endless Summer by Fennesz

In 2001 Christian Fennesz released his third full-length record Endless Summer which followed the disrupted drone and noise aestheti
hook: Twenty-five years later, Fennesz's ambient masterpiece still moves you without explaining why—emotion as texture, not narrative.
